blocks.text: This is a project that Ben M. put in front of me years back now, basically you make a compliment to the heavens that renders the starfield below that matches with the heavens above.
In the ideal embodiment would leave the user feeling like they are floating in the stars. Much of the ideas/approaches from the.

In practice this could be constructed of a upside down dome of sorts, that ends at the local horizons (maybe 20 degrees elevation?). In the pedestal that the user stands on in the middle, there is a set of high-dark-point projectors (or specialized masks like from the Universal Ring notion), that speckle a projection surface with the stars that should be where the earth is blocking, hence the name … Earth Remover.

items.text: The sky is super dark, you need a surface that is very absorbent to project on that might have special spectral properties to optimized the light that only the setup projects on it.
Projectors generally have poor “dark-point”, aka they still project some light onto the surfaces which should nominally be black.
There are other ways to do this, possibly with a very large number of small watch-form factor LCD panels. That would be a logistic and electronic nightmare, but hey, that is kinda what we do here.
